content_en:
Ba-Fadl al-Hadhrami presents the rules of taharah in Al-Muqaddimah with the clarity and conciseness that characterizes the entire text. He follows the standard Shafi'i arrangement, beginning with the classification of waters, moving through the types of impurity, and then covering wudu, ghusl, tayammum, and the removal of physical impurities.

Water, in the Shafi'i school as presented in Al-Muqaddimah, is classified into four categories. The first is mutlaq water — pure and purifying — which is the original, unaltered water suitable for all ritual purifications. The second is water mixed with a pure substance that changes its description away from pure water. The third is musta'mal water — water that has been used in an obligatory purification, which is pure but cannot be used for further obligatory purification. The fourth is najis water — water rendered impure by contact with a physical impurity in amounts or qualities that alter its characteristics, or any water below two qullahs that makes contact with any najasah.

The threshold of two qullahs (approximately 190–200 liters by most estimates) is central to Shafi'i water law. Small quantities of water (below two qullahs) become impure by mere contact with any impurity, regardless of whether the water's characteristics change. Large quantities are only impure when their color, taste, or smell changes. Ba-Fadl presents this distinction concisely, and the commentaries expand on it with the relevant hadith evidence from Sunan Abu Dawud and other collections.

The obligatory acts of wudu are six: (1) intention when beginning to wash the face, (2) washing the face including the nostrils and the area between the lips, (3) washing the hands and arms including the elbows, (4) wiping any part of the head — even a single hair in its natural position — (5) washing the feet including the ankles, and (6) maintaining sequence. The Shafi'i minimum for head-wiping distinguishes it from the Maliki requirement of full head and the Hanafi requirement of one-quarter.

The nullifiers of wudu are seven in the Shafi'i school: (1) anything exiting from the front or back passage, (2) loss of consciousness, (3) skin-to-skin contact between a man and a marriageable woman without a barrier — one of the Shafi'i school's most discussed positions — (4) touching the private parts with the palm of the hand, (5) touching another person's private parts with the palm, and two positions on touching one's own private parts. Ba-Fadl presents these concisely, while commentaries address the evidential debates.

Ghusl (major purification) is obligatory upon: ejaculation of semen, insertion, menstruation, and postnatal bleeding. The obligatory acts of ghusl are two: intention and washing the entire body surface including the hair. The prophetic description of ghusl method — as narrated by 'A'ishah in al-Bukhari and Muslim — provides the model of the sunnah method, which adds wudu, pouring water over the head three times, and then washing the body.

Tayammum in Al-Muqaddimah is treated with its conditions: absence of water within approximately one mile, or inability to use water due to illness. The method is: intention, striking clean earth with both palms, wiping the face, striking again, and wiping both hands to the wrists. The Shafi'i school requires two strikes — one for the face and one for the hands — unlike the Maliki and Hanbali positions that permit one strike for both.
content_id:
Ba-Fadhl al-Hadrami menyajikan hukum-hukum taharah dalam Al-Muqaddimah dengan kejelasan dan keringkasan yang menjadi ciri khas seluruh teks ini. Beliau mengikuti susunan standar Syafi'i, dimulai dengan klasifikasi air, kemudian jenis-jenis najasah, wudhu, mandi janabah, dan tayamum — semuanya disajikan dalam urutan yang logis dan mudah diikuti oleh pelajar pemula.

Mengenai air, mazhab Syafi'i membagi air menjadi empat kategori: air mutlak (suci dan menyucikan), air musta'mal (suci tetapi tidak menyucikan — sudah dipakai untuk bersuci), air yang bercampur benda suci sehingga mengubah sifatnya, dan air najis. Pembagian ini lebih terstruktur dari mazhab Maliki dan sedikit berbeda dari Hanafi, namun dalam praktik sehari-hari perbedaannya jarang signifikan.

Wudhu menurut mazhab Syafi'i memiliki enam fardhu: niat, membasuh muka, membasuh tangan sampai siku, mengusap sebagian kepala, membasuh kaki sampai mata kaki, dan tertib (urutan yang benar). Persyaratan tartib (urutan) ini adalah salah satu ciri khas mazhab Syafi'i yang membedakannya dari mazhab Hanafi. Ba-Fadhl menyajikan keenam fardhu ini dengan sangat jelas sehingga mudah dihafal dan dipraktikkan.

Bagian tentang mandi janabah mencakup fardhu-fardhunya — niat dan meratakan air ke seluruh tubuh termasuk rambut — serta sunnah-sunnahnya. Tayamum dibahas dengan cara yang sama: fardhu-fardhunya, kondisi-kondisi yang memperbolehkannya, dan cara melakukannya yang benar menurut mazhab Syafi'i. Seluruh pembahasan taharah ini disusun agar dapat langsung dipraktikkan oleh pelajar yang baru belajar fiqh.
content_ar:
يعرض باعلوي الحضرمي أحكام الطهارة في المقدمة بوضوح وإيجاز يُميّزان الكتاب كله، سالكاً الترتيب الشافعي المعهود: يبدأ بتصنيف المياه ثم ينتقل إلى أنواع الأنجاس ثم إلى الوضوء والغسل والتيمم وإزالة الخبث.

والمياه في المذهب الشافعي كما تُقرّرها المقدمة أربعة أصناف. الأول: الماء المطلق طاهر مطهِّر. الثاني: الماء المستعمل طاهر غير مطهِّر. الثالث: المتغيّر بنجاسة نجس مُنجِّس. الرابع: المتغيّر بطاهر كالزعفران طاهر غير مطهِّر. وقد دار خلاف واسع في المذهب حول حكم الماء القليل الذي خالطته نجاسة دون تغيير أوصافه، وتميل المقدمة إلى التشدد المعتاد في الفقه الشافعي.

وفي الوضوء تُعرض الفرائض الستة: النية، وغسل الوجه، وغسل اليدين إلى المرفقين، ومسح بعض الرأس، وغسل الرجلين إلى الكعبين، والترتيب. وتُذكر سنن الوضوء ومسنوناته مع التمييز الشافعي الدقيق بين الأركان والسنن المؤكدة وسائر الهيئات.

وتُعرض موجبات الغسل مع تمييز الحنابلة من الشافعية فيما يوجبه والتقاء الختانين. ويختص باب التيمم ببيان شروطه عند تعذّر الماء أو العجز عن استعماله.
